Description: Multi-repository static site generator purpose-built for AsciiDoc. Designed for large, versioned, component-based documentation sets common in enterprise open-source projects. Outputs static HTML; no hosted service — you own the build and deploy pipeline.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. Gate via web server (nginx basic auth, reverse proxy, Cloudflare Access, etc.).

Description: Developer-focused documentation platform with a Notion-like block editor, API reference support, and git sync. Supports both internal and external docs from a single workspace. Distinguishes itself with a clean writing experience and strong support for code-heavy technical content.
Security Notes: SAML SSO via Okta, Azure AD, or Google (all plans). Team-level RBAC on all plans. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR certified. No native 2FA mentioned — relies on IdP.

Description: Documentation theme and framework built on top of Astro. Delivers fast, accessible doc sites with built-in full-text search, i18n, versioning, and a clean default design. Rapidly gaining adoption across the JS ecosystem.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. Gate at CDN or host layer (Cloudflare Access, Vercel password, etc.).

Category: Framework, Static Site Generator, API Docs
Description: Python-native static site generator for technical documentation, blogs, and knowledge bases. Builds on free-threaded Python 3.14+ for parallel rendering with incremental, cache-compressed rebuilds. Ships with 60+ MyST-style directives and native Jupyter notebook support.
Security Notes: Unknown — insufficient public documentation on auth/gating.

Description: AI-powered coding assistant platform offering tools for code generation, explanation, enhancement, conversion, and unit test creation across 60+ programming languages. Includes sandboxed code execution. Aimed at developers looking to automate repetitive coding and documentation tasks.
Security Notes: Unknown — insufficient public documentation on auth/gating.
AI Pricing & Models: Free tier, Included in plan · Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, Other

Description: Lightweight, no-build-step documentation site generator. Docsify renders Markdown files directly in the browser at runtime — no build step needed. Popular for small project docs, internal wikis, and prototyping. Limited SEO and search compared to built SSGs.
Security Notes: Client-side renderer serving static files — no built-in auth. Must be gated at the web server or CDN layer.

Category: Platform, Framework, Static Site Generator, API Docs, Knowledge Management, AI
Description: FOSS docs-as-code CMS with Git-backed storage (local or cloud). Combines static site generation with a visual editing layer via Tina CMS integration. Supports incremental builds and MDX authoring.
Security Notes: Unknown — insufficient public documentation on auth/gating.

Description: Open-source documentation framework built and maintained by Meta. Uses React and MDX for component-driven docs. Features built-in versioning, i18n, and search. Widely adopted across the JavaScript and open-source ecosystem.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. Gate via hosting provider (Netlify password, Vercel, Cloudflare Access) or reverse proxy.

Description: The long-standing de facto standard for generating documentation from annotated source code. Supports C++, C, Java, Python, and more. Outputs HTML, PDF, and other formats directly from in-code comments and docstrings.
Authoring Workflow Model: Annotated source code, Local files + CLI
Security Notes: Generates static HTML — no built-in auth. Gate at web server layer (Apache/nginx basic auth, etc.).

Category: Platform, Framework, Static Site Generator, API Docs
Description: API documentation and SDK generation platform that reads OpenAPI specs to auto-generate reference docs and client SDKs in multiple languages. Offers hosted developer portals and Git-based collaboration workflows.
Authoring Workflow Model: AI-assisted, API Spec-driven, Git-synced, In-browser editor, Local files + CLI
Security Notes: SSO via SAML 2.0 or OIDC (Enterprise plan only — contact sales). RBAC for role-based content sections. API key injection for personalized code examples. No native 2FA/password gating.

Description: Documentation platform with a git-backed workflow, clean block editor, and support for technical writing. Handles both internal knowledge bases and external-facing product docs from one workspace. Built-in AI features for search and writing.
Auth / Security Methods: Custom Auth Backend, OIDC / OAuth, RBAC, SSO (SAML 2.0)
Security Notes: SAML SSO (Enterprise plan). Authenticated Access via custom auth backend (Ultimate plan) — delegates to your own IdP (e.g., Auth0, Okta). Email-domain SSO. No native 2FA or shared password for visitor access.

Description: Go-based static site generator known for its build speed. Widely used for documentation via themes like Docsy and Hextra. Ships as a single binary with no runtime dependencies, making CI/CD setup straightforward.

Ruby-based static site generator that powers GitHub Pages. The original modern SSG — mature theme and plugin ecosystem. Popular for project sites, OSS docs, and developer blogs. Has lost mindshare to newer JS-based SSGs but remains widely deployed.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. Gate at hosting layer (GitHub Pages org-level access, Netlify password, Cloudflare Access, reverse proxy basic auth, etc.).

Description: AI-powered Q&A layer that sits on top of your existing technical documentation. Trains on your docs to answer developer questions accurately with citations. Deployed as a chat widget, Slack bot, or API integration.
Security Notes: AI search/chat overlay — access control depends on underlying source docs and your integration config. No standalone doc-gating model.
AI Pricing & Models: Included in plan · Claude, Cohere, OpenAI, Other

Description: Modern developer documentation platform with a polished UI, MDX support, and git-based workflow. Designed for product and API docs with built-in AI search, analytics, and custom branding. Low setup friction for teams starting fresh.
Auth / Security Methods: Custom Auth Backend, OIDC / OAuth, Password (shared)
Security Notes: Password (shared), JWT, or OAuth auth launched Dec 2024. Full or partial section protection. No native SAML/SSO — relies on your own IdP via JWT or OAuth. Recent security incidents (Mar 2024, Nov 2025) prompted major hardening.
AI End-User Q&A: Built-in AI chat/search, MCP server, llms.txt provided

Description: Simple, fast static site generator written in Python and geared toward project documentation. Markdown-based with a rich plugin ecosystem. The Material for MkDocs theme makes it one of the most popular doc frameworks in open-source.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. MkDocs Material Insiders has limited access-control hooks. Otherwise gate at web server or hosting layer.
AI End-User Q&A: External / DIY integration only, Kapa.ai / Inkeep / similar embed

Description: Next.js-based documentation framework with MDX support and a clean default theme. Zero-config for React and Next.js projects, with built-in full-text search, i18n, and syntax highlighting. Gaining adoption across the JS ecosystem.

Cloud-based Component Content Management System (CCMS) for enterprise-scale structured authoring. DITA/XML-based with single-sourcing, multi-channel publishing, translation management, and review workflows built in.
Security Notes: 2FA/TOTP required by default for all users (auth app or email code). SAML SSO via Okta, JumpCloud, or Azure AD (Enterprise plan). MFA centrally enforceable on Enterprise. Strong RBAC on all plans.

Description: Hosting and CI/CD platform purpose-built for Sphinx and MkDocs documentation projects. Automatically builds and publishes docs on every git push, with versioning and PDF export. Free for open-source projects.
Auth / Security Methods: Git-provider SSO, Password (shared), SSO (SAML 2.0), Secret Link, Token / HTTP Header
Security Notes: Business plan: SSO via GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or Google Workspace. Enterprise (beta): SAML via Okta only. Sharing options: secret link, shared password, or HTTP header token with expiry. FOSS version has minimal native auth.

Description: Developer hub platform focused on API documentation and developer experience. Features interactive API explorers, changelog management, and usage analytics showing which endpoints developers use. Targeted at companies exposing APIs to external developers.
Auth / Security Methods: LDAP, OIDC / OAuth, Password (shared), RBAC, SSO (SAML 2.0)
Security Notes: SAML SSO (Enterprise): supports AD/LDAP, ADFS, G Suite, Office 365, Azure AD, OIDC, PingFederate, JumpCloud. Password for private projects. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA. Per-project public/private toggle.

Description: API documentation platform built around OpenAPI. Open-source CLI for building and linting API reference docs, plus a hosted platform with portals and access control. Known for its polished ReDoc rendering engine widely embedded in other tools.
Security Notes: SAML2 or OIDC SSO (Enterprise). RBAC with IdP group/attribute team mapping. SCIM 2.0 provisioning (beta). Audit trail. Page- and nav-level access control. SOC 2 Type II.

Description: Python's native documentation generator and the foundation of the open-source docs ecosystem. Supports reStructuredText natively and Markdown via MyST. Powers CPython, Django, NumPy, and thousands of other project docs.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. Gate at the web server or hosting layer.
AI End-User Q&A: External / DIY integration only, llms.txt provided

Description: API design and documentation platform with a visual editor for OpenAPI specs. Bridges API design and documentation — teams can design, mock, test, and publish API docs from a single workflow. Useful for teams wanting a less code-heavy approach than Redocly or Fern.
Security Notes: SAML SSO, LDAP, or Git-provider SSO (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket). Granular roles and permissions. Private and public docs hosted in one hub. Enterprise feature.

Description: Code-coupled documentation tool that embeds docs directly in the codebase and auto-updates them when code changes. Keeps internal docs in sync with the code they describe. Designed for engineering teams maintaining internal developer documentation.
Auth / Security Methods: OIDC / OAuth, On-prem / Air-gapped, SCIM, SSO (SAML 2.0)
Security Notes: SAML SSO via Okta, Azure AD, JumpCloud. SCIM provisioning. On-prem deployment including air-gapped environments. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001. Docs live in your Git repo — inherits repo-level access controls.

Description: Vue-powered static site generator from the Vue/Vite team. Fast, Markdown-first, with built-in search and a clean default theme. The natural choice for Vue ecosystem projects and widely used beyond Vue for its speed and simplicity.
Security Notes: Static site generator — no built-in auth. Gate at CDN or host layer.

Description: JetBrains documentation authoring environment available as a plugin for JetBrains IDEs. Supports Markdown and XML-based authoring with live preview and built-in doc testing. Previously available as a standalone IDE.
Security Notes: JetBrains IDE plugin that generates static output. No built-in auth — gate at hosting layer. JetBrains Space hosting may add some controls.
AI Pricing & Models: BYOK, Included in plan · Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, Self-hosted

Description: Modern static site generator built by the creators of Material for MkDocs. Designed as a next-generation docs site framework with enhanced theming, navigation, and content authoring capabilities.
Security Notes: Unknown — insufficient public documentation on auth/gating.
